_comment: WASM/binary asset manifest for GitHub Pages deployment. GPL binaries are NOT committed to source (ADR-014). See docs/PROGRESS.md for Phase B2 status.

yaneuraou._note: material variant: built-in piece-count eval (self-contained, no external .nnue file needed). Requires SharedArrayBuffer (COOP+COEP on consumer app). SRI hashes committed to engines.json.

edax._note: Emscripten ASYNCIFY build from abulmo/edax-reversi v4.4. SRI hash committed to engines.json (v0.1.4). Deployed to GitHub Pages. Data files (eval.dat ~14MB) bundled via --preload-file. No SharedArrayBuffer required (single-threaded ASYNCIFY).

files._note: Worker wrapper (scripts/edax-worker.js) — bridges postMessage <-> Emscripten stdin/stdout
Emscripten ASYNCIFY module (loaded by edax.js via importScripts)
Main WASM binary (includes eval.dat preloaded via Emscripten FS)

katago._note: No standalone WASM binary available. Best path: kaya-go/katago-onnx converts official KataGo checkpoints to fp32 ONNX format, runnable in browser via onnxruntime-web. Requires architecture change (not a stdin/stdout worker but an ONNX inference engine). Deferred.

_research.tfJsPath: Sir-Teo/web-katrain — browser KaTrain clone (WebGPU primary, WASM fallback)
_research.blocker: No drop-in WASM worker compatible with GTPAdapter stdin/stdout protocol

gnubg._note: Emscripten build from hwatheod/gnubg-web (gnubg 1.05.000 + glib 2.62.0). Uses direct _run_command() export (no ASYNCIFY — synchronous C function calls). Data bundle (~2MB): gnubg.wd neural net + gnubg_os0.bd bearoff DB + match equity tables. Output artifact 'gnubg-wasm-v1.05' deployed to GitHub Pages.
gnubg._architecture: Direct C function exports — Worker calls Module._run_command(buf) synchronously; no blocking stdin, no ASYNCIFY needed.

files._note: Emscripten glue JS (loaded by gnubg.js via importScripts)
Preloaded data bundle: gnubg.wd + bearoff DBs + METs (~2MB)

kingsrow._note: BLOCKED: KingsRow is proprietary freeware (Windows DLL only). No source code available for WASM compilation. Recommended alternative: rapid-draughts@1.0.6 (MIT, npm, TypeScript, no build required).

mortal._note: BLOCKED: Mortal is PyTorch-based (deep learning Mahjong AI). Direct WASM compilation not feasible. Options: (1) convert to ONNX and run via onnxruntime-web, (2) use a lighter rule-based Mahjong engine, (3) server-side inference with WebSocket bridge.

_research.blocker: PyTorch dependency; model size 100+ MB likely too large for browser
